{{Quote|This is not a cattle market in Shaum Hii, Lieutenant Tschel. This is the bridge of an Imperial Star Destroyer. Routine information is not—repeat, ''not''—simply shouted in the general direction of its intended recipient. Is that clear|Captain Gilad Pellaeon|<ref name="htte">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref>}}
'''Tschel''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was [[Imperial officer/Legends|military officer]] in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|military forces]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]]. Tschel had characteristic of former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] personnel who came of age after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Biography==
By [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Tschel held the rank of [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]], and was assigned as a bridge officer aboard the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], then serving as the [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] himself. In the eyes of the flagship's [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], Tschel was absurdly young to hold such an important post, with a lack of experience and self-control for which his training and enthusiasm could not compensate—but also all too typical of the young volunteers and conscripts who now made up the bulk of the Starfleet's front-line forces, and hardly to be blamed for his own lack of combat experience.

The subsequent [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] tempered and hardened Tschel and other young officers like him, but little is known of his service over the subsequent decade after the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]].

[[19 ABY/Legends|Ten years later]], Tschel served as a bridge officer aboard the ''Chimaera'' with the rank of [[Major/Legends|Major]] under Captain [[Ardiff]].<ref name="SotP" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tschel first appeared in the [[1991]] novel ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' by [[Timothy Zahn]]. According to Zahn, Tschel was designed to show the slipping quality of Imperial personnel since the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and to contrast with the competence shown by his superior, Captain Pellaeon.<ref name="20th Anniversary">''[[Heir to the Empire: The 20th Anniversary Edition]]''</ref>
